# Plan - 2026-06-23

Source: `.github/research/2026-06-22-research.md`

The 06-22 brief surfaced three items: two are already-filed issues
(#831 webfetch, #829 streaming partial save), and only one was net-
new actionable scope (streaming-error contract docs). The brief's
stronger signal was the **Carryover BLOCKER**: a 4th-consecutive
flag that 5 high-quality enhancement tickets sit without the
`auto-implement` label and the engineering queue has been at 0 open
`auto-implement` issues for a day. This plan executes the brief's
Actionable Recommendation #1 in full (backfill labels), files the
one genuinely new issue (Item #3), and leaves Item #1 / #2
untouched since they already have ticket bodies that an engineer
can pick up unchanged.

## Issues created

| # | Issue | Title | Category | Size |
|---|-------|-------|----------|------|
| 1 | #839 | [providers] Document streaming-error contract + add conformance test helper | config (docs) | small (~2h) |

Only one net-new issue this run. The brief offered three findings,
two of which already had filed issues (see "Pre-existing issues"
below). Per the role's "Do not create issues without a corresponding
research source" rule, no filler was invented to hit the 5-issue cap.
The engineering queue grew from 0 -> 8 anyway via the label backfill
described below.

#839 verified post-creation with `gh issue view 839 --json labels`:
`["documentation", "enhancement", "auto-implement"]`.

Re-filing #831 / #829 would have duplicated existing well-scoped
bodies. The pragmatic move was to label them and let the engineering
pipeline pick them up immediately.

## Implementation tasks (engineering breakdown for #839)

Mirrors the `How` section of the issue, reproduced here so
`auto-implement.yml` can dispatch the work without re-reading the
issue body.

### Task A -- `docs/PROVIDERS.md` (~40 min)

1. Read `docs/PROVIDERS.md` in full (~609 lines). Locate the natural
   insertion point: immediately after the "Provider Call Flow"
   mermaid block (line ~163) and before the "Supported Models"
   heading (line ~165).
2. Add the "Streaming error semantics" section, ~60 lines, listing
   the three rules with one-line "why" for each, a TypeScript code
   block showing the expected pattern, a "Do NOT" counter-example,
   and a "Conformance test" subsection pointing to the helper from
   Task B.
3. Link to issue #829 in the "Related" line so a reader landing on
   the doc can navigate to the orchestrator-side change.
4. ASCII only. Straight quotes. No smart dashes. The Encoding Guard
   workflow rejects U+200B.

### Task B -- `src/providers/__tests__/streaming-contract.ts` (~50 min)

1. Inspect `tests/providers/` and `src/providers/__tests__/`
   layout. Match the convention that already exists for provider
   tests in this repo. (At time of writing,
   `tests/streamingOrchestratorHeaders.test.ts` and
   `tests/core/streamingOrchestrator.fallback.test.ts` live under
   `tests/`, suggesting `tests/providers/streaming-contract.ts`
   may be the right path; verify before writing.)
2. Implement the `assertStreamingContract` helper with the three
   sub-assertions (Rule 1 throw propagation, Rule 2 final usage,
   Rule 3 negative-case swallowing detection). Signature in the
   issue body.
3. Add one test case in an existing SAP-orchestration provider test
   file that mocks `OrchestrationClient` and calls
   `assertStreamingContract` against the real provider class. Do
   NOT require live SAP credentials -- follow the existing mock
   pattern.
4. Run the full gate sequence:
   ```bash
   npm run lint
   npm run typecheck
   npm run format:check
   npm run test:coverage
   npm run build
   ```
5. CI line-coverage threshold is 40%. A docs-only patch + small
   helper + one test case should clear it comfortably.

### Suggested merge order

Single PR. The doc and the test helper are two halves of the same
contract and reviewers benefit from seeing both together. The
helper alone has no consumer; the doc alone has nothing to verify
the rules against.

## Coordination with existing tickets

- **#829** (streaming partial save): merging #839 first is fine but
  not required. The two are complementary: #829 catches the throw,
  #839 ensures the throw happens. Engineering can pick #829 first
  if it lands faster -- the doc-side contract is informational
  until a future provider violates it.
- **#831** (webfetch bound body): independent file path
  (`src/tool/tools/webfetch.ts`), no coordination needed. Brief
  Actionable Recommendation #2 notes #831 and #829 share test
  scaffolding (mock fetch throws mid-stream), so an engineer who
  picks up both can amortise setup.
- **#812** (message-builder truncation), **#810** (task subagent
  permission), **#818** (topK plumbing): all unrelated; can be
  worked in parallel.

- **Brief Recommendation #3: Demote claude-code polling**: already
  tracked by **#772** (`[config] Demote claude-code and aider to
  weekly research polling`), which is already labelled
  `auto-implement` since the 06-19 plan run. No new issue.
- **Brief Recommendation #2: Co-implement #1 and #2 to share test
  scaffolding**: noted above under Coordination. Not actionable as
  a separate ticket -- it is engineering process guidance.

## Out of scope

- aider 30-day silence: brief flagged as informational; no action
  warranted until aider produces a signal.
- claude-code 100% CHANGELOG noise this cycle: brief flagged as
  expected, confirms the demotion premise behind #772. No new
  ticket.
- kilocode v7.3.51/52 "recall-tail" feature: explicitly N/A per
  the brief because Alexi has no recall tool.

## Constitution & process check

- **SAP AI Core-First**: #839 is docs + a generic streaming-contract
  test helper. The single test case wires the SAP orchestration
  provider through it as the canonical conformant implementation.
  No parallel provider stack introduced.
- **Provider Abstraction**: the helper is provider-agnostic by
  design (takes a factory), which is exactly what the constitution
  wants for any future second provider.
- **Scope-enum compliance**: #839 will commit under `providers`
  (in the shared `cli, core, providers, config, server, agent,
  tools, ci, deps, tests` enum). #818 (topK) also uses `providers`;
  #812 uses `core`; #810 uses `tools`; #804 uses `mcp` which is NOT
  in the enum, so its PR must use `core` or `tools` instead
  (already noted in #804's issue body).

## Tasks file

A separate `tasks.md` was not produced for this run because the
single new issue (#839) is already broken down into two engineering
tasks (A, B) directly in this plan under "Implementation tasks".
The carryover issues (#818, #812, #810, #804, #831, #829) each
already carry their own detailed `How` sections written when they
were originally filed; engineering should treat the issue bodies
themselves as the tasks file.
